“Great! That’s great! Mind if I just go up and have a look in on ‘em?” they hear Uncle George asking, even as he’s moving towards the stairs. Emily’s dad follows behind George still answering George’s question about the girls, “Have you ever seen Brenna’s feet, George? No, of course not. You wouldn’t have. Now Brenna is the one about to faint as the two huddle in the coat closet. Emily whispers in her ear, “We have to make a break for it NOW before they reach my bedroom!” and Brenna gives a shivering nod.

Brenna is closest, so she fumblingly takes hold of the doorknob with both hands and opens it slowly, trying to avoid any creaks of the door hinges. They grab their things and run for the door, fling it open, and…

The practice went on for longer than anyone expected, and before long the only people left near the theater building were Brenna; her drama instructor Mr. Woodrow (who is gay and immune to the effects of the teen female foot-mania); a custodian named Guss; and two other theater students, both girls. One is a pretty 15-year-old brunette with a crazy booty but not much chest yet named Delaney. The other is a 16-year-old Asian girl named Ming with straight jet-black hair and a slender, petite, but well-proportioned body.

They are just finishing up a dress-rehearsal and were still costumed for the play when Mr. Woodrow finally felt that everyone had their lines down and they could go home for the night. Brenna and Delaney and Ming were heading back to the dressing room area, still in costume, when they surprised Gus, who was acting strange, playing with their street clothes (namely their shoes and socks) when he wasn’t even supposed to be in the girl’s dressing area. Gus always looked a little shifty, but tonight was different. He looked positively demented as he covered his face with their footwear. It was a second before he’d realized they’d stumbled in on him, and the sickly smile that snuck across Gus’s face as he eyed the girls made all three scream and slam the door on Gus and run back down the hall behind stage right. Gus burst out of the room, giving chase, while the girls all screamed and ran in the opposite direction.

11411.content:
Her heart pounding, Brenna follows the total weirdo and creepshow, Jason, through the fence, down a short municipal access road, and into the decayed, moss-coated ruins of the old Raccoon Hill cemetery. Even in the dark, the crumbling gothic tower of the massive old church stood out; the historic bell tower--long closed as a public hazard--blotting out the stars overhead with a dozen tons of stone and bronze.

They slow to a halt in the forest of old tombs and leaning crosses. Despite her slender build, Brenna was quite fit; Jason, on the other hand, was already panting from the short run. The blonde beauty arched one finely-done eyebrow at him.

"Seriously? Maybe I should have taken my chances with the net," she says. Jason scowls up at her.

"Okay, maybe I'm not so in shape, sure. But you'd be surprised how well this net works," he says, before adding "Worst case, I could have shouted and had those other guys find you."

"Thanks, I guess, for not doing that," Brenna sighed, taking the chance to size up Jason in the pale moonlight. He hadn't lost weight since graduating, looked like. He was probably 19, maybe 20 now. They'd been in school together for less than a year, but that hadn't stopped him from becoming nearly a stalker online.

She then turned away from Jason, judging him to still be a minute or two away from having recovered enough to be dangerous. From the gentle rise of Raccoon Hill, she could make out much of her neighborhood stretching away below her. Clearly, all was not well. The lights were still on in windows, and most streetlights continued to glow, but the streets were weirdly quiet. Except, of course, for the faint strains of voice carried past on the winds. Female voices, mostly. Young, frantic female voices. Laughs, screams, shouts; followed often by ominous silence or sudden, raucous jeers. It was these jeers that unsettled Brenna the most. Male voices, deep and resonant, full of animalistic exuberance. Her mind flicked back to her half-naked mother, being dragged away by those terrifying men.

"I have to find out what's going on," Brenna muttered, clenching her fists. "I won't let anyone stop me."

She turned and froze. Jason had been slowly creeping up behind her; he straightened and smiled as she turned towards him.
"I, uh. Hi! We made it!" he said cheerily, still slightly winded. "So, uh, about the thing. You know. I'm happy to help you, really. You don't want to be alone in all this, do you? I'm your guy. Your mercenary ninja. You know they were like that, right? Mercenary. Badass, right? Anyhow...about those tootsies."

Brenna sighed. Jason was a total creep, but somehow, his total creepiness seemed to have stayed constant while everyone else's went berserk. She could do worse than to have a relatively-sane ally during...whatever was going on.
On the other hand, she couldn't keep her toes from scrunching in distaste, imagining whatever weird massage Jason probably wanted to give them.

"What did you want, exactly?" she asked.

"Just a few minutes of play, here and there," he said. "Nothing, uh, nothing too weird! A little play, a little rub, maybe a liiiiiiiiiiiittle tiny eensie weeeeensie little itty bit of tickling...and only when it's safe to do! Promise, once I'm done we'll be back on the move.
Just a wee bit of tootsie-time!"

Brenna sighed. Maybe it was worth it--

"Oh, and can I tie them up?" he added rapidly. "Just the ankles. You could still clock me and untie them. It just helps the, you know. The image. Mmmmm."

He's utterly mental, the teen thought to herself. How is this my best chance at figuring out what's going on?

With a sigh, Brenna settled on her answer. She didn't have time to muck around.

11413.content:
Brenna and Jason run for it, with the home invaders doggedly in pursuit. They maintained a close enough distance to always see where the girl and the ninja costumed boy were turning to try to evade them! “There! Quick, through that chain-link fence! Quiet as you can!”, Jason hiccuped for air while first Brenna and then he pushed through to the tranquility of the golf course at night time, “There! There! The sand pit! Lay down flat as you can! Don’t move until I tell you the coast is clear!”, hissed Jason, who hid behind a squat tree that branched off in two thick branches like horns.

Brenna is so frightened, she runs down the gentle slope to the sand trap and practically dives in, laying belly down and not daring to look up over the grassy edge of the pit. She lies there, with the sounds of the insects seeming deafeningly loud, listening for Jason or for their pursuers. Seconds feel like minutes and her whole body is tingling with nervous energy as she waits. And waits.

Brenna is just about to lift her head slightly to get a peek at things when suddenly a bright flashlight beam crosses over the pit and back again, before moving on to other possible hiding places.

Where’d you go, kitty? I want to take you home with me! Yes! Damn it! Where are you!”, the figure was yelling by the time he was finished, in a rage at losing his sweet teen quarry. He rounded the corner and then the girls saw him cut across the deserted street still mumbling and cursing.

And still Rachel made the two of them wait until she finally gave Brenna the okay and pointed to a shortcut through old man Dandridge’s side yard that would take them right to Rachel’s back yard.

Brenna tried to tell Rachel that she would rather go to her own house and check on her Mom, but Rachel wouldn’t let her speak, and they quickly scampered from their cover down the sidewalk and in between yards, trying to get to Rachel’s house.

Mean old Mr. Dandridge forces the increasingly frightened teens through his front hall past the stairs and into the kitchen. “Good Lord above! You two are noisier than my chickens! Sorry I had to do that. But I can’t have just any old body come in here, spoiling a good old fashioned egg inspection. Now could I? Heh, heh, heh! Now then, you two just make yourselves comfortable while I put old Rufus out, and maybe find some things to make your visit more…exciting for us all”, and with that, old man Dandridge flips off the light, leaving the teen cuties bound naked and helpless in total darkness.

Brenna felt her arms again pulled towards the ceiling. "Gnnnnnnt pffffffffllllllllll" she hissed. It felt weird standing naked in a pitch dark room butt to butt with her friend Rachel. 114213.content:
Dandridge's house was exactly as they'd expected: Filthy, cluttered, and odorous. Brenna wrinkled her nose, shocked anyone could live like this.

But both she and Rachel gasped when Dandridge pressed a few keys on his ancient, oversized television remote control, and part of the stained wall swung inwards, revealing a secret passage.

"In you girls go," he ordered, with his dog still nipping at their heels. The terrified girls quickly followed, entering the small passage and emerging in a dark, dank chamber apparently at the heart of his creaking residence. He followed, still holding the dog and gun, and the door swung shut behind him.

Slowly, the girl's eyes grew accustomed to the light, just as Brenna felt the cold steel of the shotgun against her back. "Right this way, missy," he ordered, and she followed his lead into what she only noticed was a full-sized cage in the corner of the room. He swung it shut and a heavy, rust lock fell into place.

Rachel spun on her heel, her eyes wide as she realized what was in the room with them.

This was no ordinary room, but an entire torture chamber that would have made a medieval tyrant blush. There were heavy wooden stocks, cages, a rack, dangling chains, and other machines and devices she didn't recognize. The walls hung heavy with brushes, whips, straps, bonds, handcuffs; every tool of bondage a girl could imagine, and many only a sadistic man could. A low rack of coals glowed near one set of stocks, and an icemaker rumbled happily near another. Rachel's eyes ended up on the rusty bedframe in the far corner, with a stained mattress on top, and heavy manacles on each post.

It was Brenna who noticed the cameras mounted all over the room; on walls, tripods, over the bed, and the computer humming in the corner near a stack of external drives.

Dandridge grinned. "Truth is, girly girl, I don't really care about eggs."

He walked over to the monitor, where users from all over the world were logging on for Dandrige's popular annual webshow. Each year, thousands more perverts logged onto the premium channel, paying several thousand dollars to watch live, non-consensual teen tickling and torture. 115.content:
It was the last night she had to finish the research report she'd put off all week, and Brenna was feeling the strain. She sat in a quiet corner of the main library downtown, surrounded by her binders and splayed-open books, her laptop glowing softly beside her. She sat with her legs folded in her chair, her shoes neatly sitting on the floor, and leaned forwards over the table.

She'd begged and begged to be allowed into AP Chem, even though it was customarily reserved for seniors alone, and finally had earned admittance as the only sophomore in the class. Now, there was no way she was going to get anything but a solid grade. Still, it would probably have been smarter to have planned out the work ahead of time. She had never seen a project so demanding as the one that laid murdered before her, papers and pencils strewn violently about it.

"Gotta focus," she hissed softly, tucking an errant strand of hair behind her ear in spite of the dozen hair ties looped over her left wrist. "Gotta finish." She'd even taken the desperate step of putting her phone on silent. As much as Madison might want to talk about her latest boyfriend, tonight was a study night.

By the time she glanced at the clock, the girl inhaled sharply. It was already after 10! The library was probably closing soon.

In fact, why wasn't it closed already? Brenna lifted her head and glanced around. Nobody had been in the reading room in at least an hour, and in theory nothing had changed since the sun went down. But something was....different, somehow. There was a strange energy in the air, like on Halloween night. She'd been up all day, but she didn't feel tired at all.

"I guess that must be the adrenaline of learning," the girl chuckled, closing up several of the textbooks around her. Then, she cocked her head.

Was it her imagination, or did she just hear something....curious?

She rose from the chair and crossed to the window. Below, the street was strangely quiet. Normally there were quite a few people milling up and down the street--this wasn't the commercial part of downtown, but still, it was a dense area. Then, she saw a woman jogging below. Except, Brenna realized, she wasn't jogging. She was sprinting, fast. As if she was running from something. The woman darted sideways down an alley and out of sight, and Brenna felt a tinge of nerves.

Brenna returned to her backpack and removed her phone. Strangely, the service was down. She slid it into her back pocket and frowned. Then, she leaned out of the reading room. There was a noise echoing from down the hall, in the direction of the lobby. A crash, and what might have been...a scream?

Brenna froze. Yes, there was definitely something echoing. Muffled sounds, perhaps from downstairs? The girl froze, wishing more than ever that her phone was working.

"Help!" came a faint, female voice. A moment later, there was a slam--like a door--and then silence.

Brenna swallowed hard. She wasn't the kind of girl to do nothing in an emergency. But what should she do?

She wasn’t sure yet if it might be the start of the town’s yearly feet fest or just your average everyday assault and battery going on in one of the rooms downstairs. What Brenna did know was that either way, the logical best thing she could do right now was get her shoes and bookbag and try to sneak out of the library as quickly and quietly as possible. Maybe outside she’d get her phone signal back and she could call for help. She knew that she saw at least two men chasing the girl, and likely more from the sounds of things.

So Brenna grabbed her things and tightly tied her Converse sneakers in case she’d have to try to outrun someone. It appeared her best bet was to take the emergency stairs down to the lobby from the second floor study wing. The lobby of the library was mostly windows, and if she took the main flight of stairs down, she’d be a sitting duck!

But the side stairs took her down to the Exit door out to the back parking lot of the library. She had no way of knowing what she’d be opening the door to back here, as the huge windowed section of the building didn’t extend this far back. Brenna just prayed that her bicycle was still chained up on the front left side of the library entrance. On her bike, it was only a few minutes home. It’s take three times as long if she had to walk it.

Confused, she lifted her head from her pillow, trying to catch the unexpected noise again--and then, there it was. A faint, muffled cry, followed by what almost sounded like giggles. A moment later there was quiet thud, then what might have been heavy footsteps?

Now Alex sat up entirely. She was in her usual nightwear--an oversized tank-top and a pair of boyshorts--and she swung her legs from the covers, creeping to her bedroom door, grabbing a dark-gray hoodie along the way and slinging it over her shoulders.

Peering down the hall, the light was off in her little sister, Sophia's, room, but an orange glow slipped out from around the edges of Holly's door. It was, for the moment, quiet, and Alex was about to assume it was nothing when she heard the noise again--a heavy thump this time, followed by a short, clipped laugh. Alex groaned. Holly was 16 now and increasingly boy-crazy; she was probably flirting with some loser from the football team on Facetime. Irritated, she straightened and strode down the hall, putting her hand on the knob, and turning.

Only at the last second did she realize she shouldn't just barge into her sister's room, even to complain, unannounced. She stopped, sighed, and inhaled to speak when she heard her sister's familiar voice gasp out, "Please, no--" before letting out a muffled whimper.

So when he looked across the crowded train one day and saw a gorgeous young girl twisting helpless, surrounded by two fiendish men who happily were groping her tight body, he felt a surge all through his body that he hadn't felt in decades. He didn't care what they charged: as he watched the tender girl twisting and struggling in their grip, her tiny feet kicking in her sneakers, he knew he had to touch her.

He pushed his way to the front of the line and nearly threw himself at the girl, until he was standing right before where she struggled. He looked up at her cute face; even in her alarm, through the gag, she was adorable.

One of the gross men selling the girl out reached under her legs and pulled them forwards, so her feet and ankles were dangling right in front of Sean. "Fifty bucks," he said. Sean was entranced: Jenna struggled to kick, but the zip ties around her slim ankles did too great a job restraining her.

Sean pushed a wad of bills into the man's hand and reached out, seizing Jenna's feet.

"Don't start my time yet," he said. "Her shoes are still on."

"Well, let's change that," the men said with a laugh, and six hands quickly went to work against Jenna's best twists and squirms. In only a few seconds, her shoes were off--tossed to the jeering crowd of men--and only a few seconds later, her socks as well. As the teen's bare feet slid into view, the crowd of men went wild with cheers.
"Oh, yeah, there they are!"
"Oh yes, little girl, don't hide those feet!"
"Now those are what I'm talking about!"
"You better believe I'm about to wreck those soles!"

Sean lifted Jenna's ankles up. They were still zip-tied just above her ankle bone, but her toes were free. She curled and flexed them in panic, giving the men an impromptu sole-show that only served to drive them more wild.

"They're bare now," one of the men said. "So, start your time now, pervo. What's it going to be?"

Sean looked up at Jenna, making eye contact with the teenaged beauty. She shook her head at him, begging through the gag.

1421.content:
It was an amazing thing to see. What started out as your typical Friday night high school football game gradually turned into complete chaos right before Emma’s dumbfounded eyes.

She’d been lucky to be away from the cursed town last year, which would have been her first year as prey during the often unexpected outbreaks of foot loving hysteria among the town males. Of course, she’d hear the stories, but she’d been shielded from the insanity by her doting parents.

Even earlier in the day, she’d seen an uptick of playful tickling around school, invariably male-on-female. Sometimes even teachers and staff teasing the girls. It all seemed quite inappropriate to Emma (who also secretly dreaded even the mildest mention of tickling, let alone the actual act). Luckily, she and the rest of the cheer team had been given half day passes to get in one last practice to nail their routine before the big game with visiting Forester High.

Shortly before the game, when Coach Mathews was giving them his pep talk, he seemed to keep making references to “being on their toes tonight” and “putting their best foot first” having “lovely arches” and other odd ways of motivating them.

There were minor outbreaks as the game got under way. A random local fan would run down to the opposing side’s cheer squad and literally drag her away in front of the whole stadium. Other men, who were still barely able to fight the effects of the curse would drag them away to safety, but things went from tense to outright frightening during the halftime show. The cheerleaders were actually enjoying a break while the bands and acts were on the field. The girls were on their own recognizance for another fifteen or so minutes, before they’d be expected to assemble in their locker room and get ready for the second half.

Unfortunately, there never was a second half, as the uniforms of the Forester High girl’s band members was enough to send one after the other of the local males into a frothing foot rage, and soon they were descending from the stands onto the field, hungry for the soles of those cute young high school girls from Forester. Unlike the usual localized outbreaks, this one involved visiting males, who weren’t seemingly under the effects of the beginning of the bizarre three day outbreak (yet). Soon, fights broke out, as the coaches and football players and fans from the rival team started trying to get the locals off of their girls. Then, things just seemed to feed off the chaos and get even crazier. Males started going after the girls from the local high’s band, too.

As things got completely out of control, Emma (and possibly a friend or two from the cheer team) are heading in the general direction of the cheer team’s locker rooms sipping soft drinks. With people running around all around her and the craziness in the air getting thicker, Emma makes her way -

This only served to make the young ticklish girls even more excited, so she tried to calm them down, telling them they'd be safe when they got home, and that Casey was welcome to stay with them and ride things out.

The car had to pass through a neighborhood caught up at the height of the foot frenzy, and Emma and Casey sneaked peeks out the windows, mortified to see other girls and women running around screaming in their sleepwear or even less as crazed-looking males chased hungrily after them. Emma’s Mom again admonished them to keep their heads down, and eventually Emma knew where they were and that they were almost home. She heard Mom activate the garage door and everyone breathed a sigh of relief as they unbuckled their seat-belts while the garage door shut behind them. Safety at last!
